reasoning: Technical axis holds up well: 1px hard outlines are present and consistent across all zoom panels, shading uses 2-3 hard tones (no AA visible), and all pixels are grid-snapped — that earns a solid 7/10 on the 40% technical weight. Emotional axis is weaker (score ~5.5/10 on 60% weight): the palette is dominated by muted browns/oranges and a mid-tone green with a white chest panel. While brown + green is nature-appropriate for Hyrule, the overall impression skews earthy and rugged rather than bright/warm/charming. The reference Denzi armor pieces show snappier contrast and cleaner read-at-a-glance silhouettes. Compared to the Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom toy-diorama aesthetic — which calls for saturated yellows, pinks, vibrant blues, and friendly rounded forms — this design lacks those accent saturations and feels more generic fantasy-RPG than Hyrule-charming. The silhouette at 1× zoom is somewhat ambiguous. Combined weighted score: 0.4×7 + 0.6×5.5 ≈ 6.1, rounded to 6.
